systems.name: Upper-Structure Substitution Lattice
systems.summary: The book's central system: for each base chord type (major7, minor7, dominant7, half-diminished, altered dominant), the book maps a specific set of upper-structure arpeggios or triads, each entered from a precisely named scale degree of the base chord. The substitution choice — not the root-position arpeggio — determines harmonic color.

members.name: Major 7 chord
members.summary: Cmaj7-type targets; substitutions include minor triad on the 3rd (Em over Cmaj7) and Am7 arpeggio (relative minor).

members.name: Minor 7 chord
members.summary: Dm7-type targets; substitutions include Fmaj7 (relative major from b3), minor triad on the 5th, and augmented triad on b3.

members.name: Dominant 7 chord
members.summary: G7-type targets; substitutions include half-diminished from 3, diminished from b9/3/5/b7, minor triads on 5 or b9, and tritone-sub arpeggio.

members.name: Half-diminished chord
members.summary: Bm7b5-type targets; substitutions include maj7 from b5 and m(add9) from b3.

members.name: Altered dominant
members.summary: G7alt-type targets; substitutions include Gaug7, Bmaj7#5, Abm/maj7 arpeggios extracted from the altered scale, and m(add9) from b9.

modification_rules.name: Minor triad on the 3rd of a major chord
modification_rules.summary: Place a minor triad rooted on the 3rd of any major chord (e.g., Em over Cmaj7).

references.quote_excerpt: play a minor triad starting on the 3rd of a major chord

modification_rules.name: Minor triad on the 5th of a minor chord
modification_rules.summary: Place a minor triad rooted on the 5th of any minor chord (e.g., Am over Dm7).

references.quote_excerpt: minor triad starting on the 5th of a minor chord

modification_rules.name: Minor triad on the 5th of a dominant chord
modification_rules.summary: Place a minor triad rooted on the 5th of any dominant chord (e.g., Dm over G7) — Wes Montgomery move.

references.quote_excerpt: minor triad starting on the 5th of a dominant chord
Dm over G7 is a technique Wes Montgomery

modification_rules.name: Minor triad on the b9 of an altered dominant
modification_rules.summary: Place a minor triad rooted on the b9 (e.g., Abm over G7) for altered color.

references.topic: Minor triads over altered dominant (b9 and #9)
references.quote_excerpt: minor triad starting on the b9 of a dominant chord

modification_rules.name: Half-diminished arpeggio from 3rd of dominant
modification_rules.summary: Play a half-diminished arpeggio rooted on the 3rd of a dominant chord (Bm7b5 over G7) to bring out the 9 sound.

references.quote_excerpt: half-diminished arpeggio from the 3rd of a dominant chord
half-diminished arpeggio from the 3rd of a dominant chord

modification_rules.name: Diminished arpeggio from b9/3/5/b7 of dominant
modification_rules.summary: Play a diminished arpeggio rooted on the b9, 3, 5, or b7 of a dominant (Abdim7 over G7) for the 7b9 sound.

references.quote_excerpt: diminished arpeggio from the b9, 3, 5, or b7

modification_rules.name: Maj7 arpeggio from b3 of minor7 (relative-major sub)
modification_rules.summary: Play a maj7 arpeggio rooted on the b3 (relative major) of a minor7 — Fmaj7 over Dm7, attributed to Wes Montgomery.

references.topic: Major triad sub for minor chords
references.quote_excerpt: major triad or maj7 chord on the third of a major chord
Wes Montgomery phrase that uses an Fmaj7 arpeggio over Dm7

modification_rules.name: Maj7 arpeggio from b5 of half-diminished
modification_rules.summary: Play a maj7 arpeggio rooted on the b5 of a half-diminished (Fmaj7 over Bm7b5) for the #11 tension.

references.quote_excerpt: maj7 chord over a half-diminished chord, starting on the b5

modification_rules.name: Augmented triad/maj7#5 from b3 of minor
modification_rules.summary: Play an augmented triad or maj7#5 arpeggio rooted on the b3 of a minor chord (Gaug over Em7) for the minor/major-7 sound.

references.quote_excerpt: augmented triad or maj7#5 arpeggio, both built on the b3

references.topic: Pattern 70 – Augmented Sub over minor chord
references.quote_excerpt: augmented triad or augmented major 7 arpeggio

modification_rules.name: Augmented arpeggio over altered dominant
modification_rules.summary: An augmented 7th arpeggio or triad brings out the altered sound on a dominant chord; for tritone-sub #11 dominants, build augmented from the original V7alt root.

references.quote_excerpt: augmented 7th arpeggio or triad is a convenient way
Db7#11 is a substitution for G7alt, you can use a G augmented triad

modification_rules.name: m(add9) arpeggio from b9 of altered dominant
modification_rules.summary: Play an m(add9) arpeggio rooted on the b9 of an altered dominant (Fm(add9) over E7#9).

references.quote_excerpt: m(add9) over altered dominant chords, starting on the b9

modification_rules.name: m(add9) arpeggio from b3 of half-diminished
modification_rules.summary: Play an m(add9) arpeggio rooted on the b3 of a half-diminished (Dm(add9) over Bm7b5).

references.quote_excerpt: m(add9) arpeggio over half-diminished chords, starting on the b3

modification_rules.name: Tritone-substitute dominant arpeggio
modification_rules.summary: Replace a dominant chord with the arpeggio of its tritone substitute (Bb7 over E7).

references.quote_excerpt: tritone substitution by playing a Bb7 arpeggio over E7

modification_rules.name: Harmonic minor / Phrygian dominant over V7 of minor ii-V
modification_rules.summary: Over the V7 of a minor ii-V-I, play tonic harmonic minor (or equivalently Phrygian dominant from V's root) for 7b9/b13.

references.quote_excerpt: tonic harmonic minor scale over the V7alt chord
tonic harmonic minor scale over any dominant chord

systems.name: Bebop Scale Family with Metric Guarantee
systems.summary: The book defines four bebop scale variants — each constructed by inserting one chromatic passing tone into a parent modal scale — and prescribes a single rhythmic rule: starting the scale on a downbeat and on a chord tone guarantees all chord tones land on downbeats and non-chord tones on upbeats.

members.name: Major bebop scale
members.summary: Major scale + b6 chromatic passing tone.

members.name: Bebop melodic minor scale
members.summary: Melodic minor + b6 chromatic passing tone.

modification_rules.name: Bebop scale construction (mode + chromatic passing tone)
modification_rules.summary: Construct a bebop scale by adding exactly one chromatic passing tone to a 7-note parent mode; each variant has a specific parent mode and added pitch.

references.quote_excerpt: 8-note scale ... adding a chromatic passing note
Mixolydian mode with a descending chromatic note
Dorian mode with a descending chromatic note
major scale with a descending chromatic note
melodic minor scale + b6

traversal_rules.name: Downbeat-chord-tone entry rule (metric guarantee)
traversal_rules.summary: Enter a bebop scale run on a downbeat AND on a chord tone (1, 3, 5, or b7); all subsequent chord tones then fall on downbeats and passing tones on upbeats.

references.quote_excerpt: start the bebop scale on a downbeat and on a chord tone
as long as you start the bebop scale on a downbeat and on a chord note

traversal_rules.name: Slide-based fingering for chromatic passing tone
traversal_rules.summary: On guitar, descending bebop-scale lines use slides to reposition the fretting hand across the inserted chromatic note rather than fingering each pitch discretely.

references.quote_excerpt: play the bebop scale on guitar (descending). It involves some slides

systems.name: Approach and Enclosure Vocabulary
systems.summary: A small set of melodic devices for approaching target chord/scale tones: diatonic enclosure (one step above, one step below, then target), chromatic approach from a semitone below, arpeggio-from-below (the Honeysuckle Rose move), and doubling-notes ornamentation from Pat Metheny.

members.name: Diatonic enclosure
members.summary: Three-note approach: scale-step above target, scale-step below target, target.

members.name: Chromatic approach from below
members.summary: Single semitone below the target chord tone, used heavily in gypsy jazz.

members.name: Arpeggio-from-below (Honeysuckle Rose motif)
members.summary: Target a scale degree by playing a triad ascending into it from below.

members.name: Doubling notes (Metheny)
members.summary: Repeat (double) selected notes within a scale, arpeggio, or pattern as an ornament.

modification_rules.name: Diatonic enclosure targets a chord tone
modification_rules.summary: Apply diatonic enclosure to a chord-tone target — 9 above, 7 below, then 1 — as the foundational bebop entry pattern.

references.quote_excerpt: one note above (the 9), then one note below (the 7), and finally your target

modification_rules.name: Chromatic approach below each triad tone
modification_rules.summary: Approach each note of a target triad from one chromatic semitone below (gypsy-jazz idiom).

references.quote_excerpt: Each note of the F triad ... approached from a chromatic note below

modification_rules.name: Arpeggio-from-below targets any scale tone
modification_rules.summary: Target any scale degree by playing an ascending triad into it from below; generalized from the Honeysuckle Rose motif to any major-scale tone.

references.quote_excerpt: the 3rd note of the scale (2) is targetted by a triad from below
targetting scale notes with an arpeggio from below

modification_rules.name: Doubling-notes ornament (universal)
modification_rules.summary: Apply the doubling-notes ornament to any scale, arpeggio, or pattern — a Pat Metheny-derived universal coloration.

references.quote_excerpt: doubling notes ... applied to every scale, arpeggio, or pattern

preferences.name: Practice every pattern through the circle of fifths
preferences.summary: The book's universal practice prescription: drill any pattern through all twelve keys via the circle of fifths to acquire every fingering position, over a backing track.

references.quote_excerpt: playing it through the circle of fifths
Put on a backing track and start improvising
